description:
**Status: shipped as the foundation slice of Phase 1 (bl-f021). The integration into `Store::discover` / `bl init` / `bl claim` was split off into a separate child ball; see the linked sibling.**

First implementation slice of bl-f021. Lands the *foundation* of the nested-XDG layout (SPEC-clone-layout ¬ß3 / ¬ß4 / ¬ß6 post-bl-e9c2) ‚Äî the seven pure modules every later integration slice rests on. No behavior change for existing clones: nothing is wired into `Store::discover`, `bl init`, or `bl claim` yet.

This ball was originally scoped to bundle foundation + integration into one coherent slice (the original ball description argued against splitting). SPEC-clone-layout was rewritten in bl-e9c2 to drop hashing entirely in favour of transparent percent-encoded paths and a nested per-clone tree, and to add a fourth config file (`clone.json`). Re-planning against that rewrite revealed the integration is a sweeping refactor of `Store::discover` and its callers (state-repo, init, claim, worktree, every conformance test) that warrants its own ball. The foundation alone is large enough and self-contained enough to ship.

## Shipped in this ball

Seven new modules, 100% line coverage, clippy clean, all under the 300-line cap:

1. **`encoding`** ‚Äî URL canonicalization + RFC 3986 percent-encoding + nested clone path. Pure functions. Frozen golden vectors per ¬ß14.2. Matches the ¬ß10 hand-operable shell sequence byte-for-byte. Constant `ENC_BALLS_TASKS` = `"balls%2Ftasks"`.

2. **`xdg_paths`** ‚Äî flat per-host layout under `~/.local/state/balls/{trackers,worktrees,claims,locks,plugins-auth}/` and `~/.config/balls/<nested>/`. `XdgBases::from_env` + pure `from_strings` for testability without env mutation (bl-bfa8). `tracker_checkout`, `own_tracker_checkout`, `PerClonePaths`, `clone_json_path` are the call surface.

3. **`tracker_json`** ‚Äî strict pointer-only redirect schema (¬ß6.1 / ¬ß14.6). `state_url` required, `state_branch` optional. `deny_unknown_fields`. Presence-or-absence is the bootstrap signal (¬ß5); `read_optional` returns `Ok(None)` on missing.

4. **`layered_fields`** ‚Äî shared field types for the layered config block: `Integrate`/`IntegrateMode` (replaces `Delivery`/`DeliveryMode` with `direct`/`forge-pr`), `ReviewBlock` (replaces `ReviewConfig` with `gate_command`). Legacy field-name values (`local-squash`, `deferred`, `pre_check`) are explicitly rejected per ¬ß14.16 / ¬ß14.17. The legacy types stay where they are.

5. **`project_config`** (extended) ‚Äî added the five SPEC ¬ß6.2 second-block optional layered defaults (`integrate`, `review`, `require_remote_on_*`). `Option<>`-typed so the precedence merger can distinguish "explicitly set" from "absent ‚Äî fall through." `skip_serializing_if = None` so existing project.json files round-trip byte-identically.

6. **`repo_json`** ‚Äî per-code-repo config (¬ß6.3). Primary owner of layered fields plus four repo-only fields. Two read-time trip-wires per ¬ß6.5 / ¬ß6.7: tracker-scope fields (`version`/`id_length`/`min_bl_version`/`plugins`) abort the read; the pre-revision `target_branch` field aborts. Lenient on truly-unknown fields per ¬ß6.9. Hand-written `Default` impl returns SPEC built-ins (the derived Default's `bool::default()` would have given `false` for `auto_fetch_on_ready` and `protected_main` ‚Äî caught by the merger tests).

7. **`clone_json`** ‚Äî per-on-disk-checkout override (¬ß6.4). Owns `stealth`/`tasks_dir` (¬ß4.1 retires the previous revision's "synthetic origin-key" fallback). Carries optional overrides for every layered field. Reuses `repo_json::check_forbidden_fields` for the same ¬ß6.5 trip-wires under its own file label.

8. **`effective_config`** ‚Äî three-layer precedence merger (¬ß6.5 / ¬ß14.8). Returns one `EffectiveConfig` from `(project, repo, clone)`. The only seam the binary should consult for layered fields ‚Äî reaching into individual layers directly bypasses the rule.

## What's NOT in this ball (deferred to the integration ball)

- `Store::discover` rewrite to use the new layout first / dual-read for legacy
- `bl init` rewrite to write XDG-only (no on-main writes, no .gitignore entry)
- `bl claim` worktree relocation to `~/.local/state/balls/worktrees/<nested>/<id>/`
- Stealth wiring through `clone.json` (replacing `.balls/local/tasks_dir`)
- Dual-read of pre-XDG (`.balls/config.json` on main) and bl-fc50 (hashed XDG) layouts
- Conformance tests ¬ß14.1, ¬ß14.3, ¬ß14.4, ¬ß14.7, ¬ß14.10, ¬ß14.11, ¬ß14.12, ¬ß14.13, ¬ß14.15, ¬ß14.19, ¬ß14.20 (anything that requires the discover/init/claim seams to be on the new layout)

Conformance tests ¬ß14.2, ¬ß14.6, ¬ß14.8, ¬ß14.9 (encoding, tracker.json strict, three-way precedence, tracker-scope rejection) are *de facto* satisfied by the module-level tests on `encoding`, `tracker_json`, `effective_config`, `repo_json`/`clone_json`. The integration ball will add the end-to-end fixtures.

## Why split now

The integration is genuinely a sweeping refactor ‚Äî `Store::discover` is hot (every `bl` invocation runs it), every conformance test fixture assumes the legacy layout, and many call sites consult `store.balls_dir()` for in-repo paths. The bundling argument in the SPEC ¬ß15 paragraph (and in this ball's original description) holds for the original three-commit attempt, where most of the work *was* the integration. With the foundation modules built out as a separate, internally-coherent layer, the integration becomes a focused diff against a stable seam.

The integration ball can land as a single slice in its own right: it consumes the seven modules here as a black box, rewires `Store::discover` + `bl init` + `bl claim`, ports the conformance suite to the new layout, and ships dual-read for legacy clones.

## Conformance tests gated by this ball

¬ß14.2 (golden URL encoding vectors), ¬ß14.6 (tracker.json strict), ¬ß14.8 (three-way precedence), ¬ß14.9 (tracker-scope field rejection), ¬ß14.16 / ¬ß14.17 (new field-name acceptance + legacy-name rejection on freshly-written files). All asserted via module-level tests on `encoding`, `tracker_json`, `effective_config`, `layered_fields`, `repo_json`, `clone_json`.
title: Phase 1: dual-read XDG layout, bl init writes XDG only
